mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity and robust economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC of INDPRO growth with 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.05 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, the labor market becomes tight, wage pressures rise and future earnings growth is constrained, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the cycle matures.
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 5.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or mean return is statistically significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising federal funds rate and falling unemployment indicates monetary tightening in a strong economy, which historically precedes equity market declines as discount rates rise and earnings growth slows.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * UNRATE change) in a predictive regression yields a coefficient with p-value > 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window, or its out-of-sample Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment with FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25%, rising retail sales signal strong consumer spending that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%, the Rank IC of RSAFS growth with 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive, or the average return difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth periods is not statistically different from zero at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates broad economic expansion, which supports higher corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate shows no positive correlation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation rises above 2.5%, it erodes purchasing power and forces expectations of central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as risk premiums rise.
failure_condition: After CPI YoY rises above 2.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in payroll employment and industrial production signal robust economic momentum that boosts earnings expectations, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and INDPRO increase month-over-month
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO rise in the same month, the average subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is not positive, or the proportion of positive returns is less than 55%, or the joint signal does not outperform individual signals in a predictive test.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession housing recovery when housing starts rise from trough levels below 800k annualized, growth in housing starts drives construction employment and wealth effects, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: post-recession housing recovery (HOUST rising from low levels < 800k annualized)
failure_condition: In periods where HOUST is rising from below 800k units, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the predictive rank IC is below 0.04.
